message:
I've never been the "organized one" in my friend group—ask anyone who's seen my junk drawer. But last spring when a tornado warning popped up, I actually *had* a plan. Flashlights? Check. Bottled water? Somehow yes. Battery-powered radio that I remembered to test? I was genuinely shocked at myself.

My neighbor knocked on the door asking where to go, and I didn't panic. I just... knew. Turns out, a little calm prep earlier in the year meant I could actually *be* there for someone instead of scrambling.

It felt kind of nice, honestly. Not in a smug way—just in a "I've got this" way that I'd never felt before.

Have you ever surprised yourself by being ready when it counted?
